summary: Core login controls are present but the artifact contains a duplicate password element, some brittle locators, and missing validation/error elements needed for negative login scenarios.

issues.message: Two elements represent the same password field using different locators (placeholder and id). This creates ambiguity in automation.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the stronger locator using the stable id attribute and remove the placeholder-based locator.

issues.message: Element name is overly technical and not business-friendly.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename to username_field to align with business terminology and framework naming conventions.

issues.message: Element name uses a technical control term rather than a business-oriented name.
Name is slightly awkward and inconsistent with common UI naming conventions.
Locator relies only on placeholder text which is brittle and may change with UI text updates or localization.
issues.suggested_fix: Prefer a stable attribute such as id, form control name, or aria-labelledby if consistently mapped.

issues.message: Locator assumes the presence of an ion-icon with aria-label='home', but the captured shadow DOM shows aria-label='close', indicating a mismatch and potential locator failure.
issues.suggested_fix: Use a locator targeting the specific button position or a stable attribute such as an aria-label on the button, data-test attribute, or a reliable icon identifier.

issues.message: Locator depends on icon aria-label 'arrow back', but the captured shadow DOM indicates aria-label='close', suggesting the locator may not match the actual element.
issues.suggested_fix: Use a more reliable locator such as a unique attribute, data-test id, or a positional relationship within the header container.

issues.message: Locator depends on visible text 'SIGN IN' while the element attribute contains text='Login'. Text-based locators may break if label changes.
issues.suggested_fix: Use a stable attribute or combine tag and attribute conditions such as ion-button with text()='SIGN IN' or a dedicated test id.

issues.message: Observed workflow includes login failure scenarios for incorrect and blank credentials, but no error or validation message elements were captured.
issues.suggested_fix: Add elements such as login_error_message, username_required_message, and password_required_message if present on the UI.

approved_elements.name: username_field
approved_elements.description: Field where the user enters the username.

approved_elements.name: password_field
approved_elements.description: Field where the user enters the account password.

approved_elements.name: sign_in_button
approved_elements.description: Primary button used to submit login credentials.

approved_elements.name: home_button
approved_elements.description: Floating action button that navigates the user to the home page.

approved_elements.name: back_button
approved_elements.description: Floating action button that navigates back to the home page.
